issuesFound.title: Toggle function `open` in `mobileNav` does not update `aria-expanded` on trigger element if `_trigger` is null
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module calls `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` without verifying `_trigger` exists. If `_trigger` is null (e.g., if `init` failed to find it but `open` is called programmatically), this will throw an error or fail silently, leaving the state inconsistent for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1677: if (!_trigger) return; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `close` in `mobileNav` does not update `aria-expanded` on trigger element if `_trigger` is null
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module calls `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` without verifying `_trigger` exists. If `_trigger` is null, the ARIA state is not updated, causing screen readers to announce the menu as open when it is visually closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1704: if (!_trigger) return; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `_openInstance` in `dropdownMenu` does not verify `inst.trigger` exists before setting `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` calls `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` without checking if `inst.trigger` is valid. If the instance is corrupted or the trigger removed, this fails to update the ARIA state, misleading screen reader users about the dropdown's open state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935: if (!inst.trigger) return; setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `_closeInstance` in `dropdownMenu` does not verify `inst.trigger` exists before setting `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` calls `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` without checking if `inst.trigger` is valid. Failure to update this attribute leaves the dropdown announced as expanded even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: if (!inst.trigger) return; setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `open` in `faqAccordion` does not verify `item.trigger` exists before setting `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` calls `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` without verifying `item.trigger` exists. If the item object is malformed, the ARIA state is not updated, causing accessibility tree inconsistencies for screen reader users navigating the FAQ.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2564: if (!item.trigger) return; setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `close` in `faqAccordion` does not verify `item.trigger` exists before setting `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `faqAccordion` calls `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` without verifying `item.trigger` exists. This omission prevents the correct announcement of the collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2592: if (!item.trigger) return; setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in `siteSearch` `_updateStatus` does not announce result count changes via dedicated live region write in all paths
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateStatus` function in `siteSearch` updates `_statusEl.textContent` based on count, but `_statusEl` is created dynamically in `init`. If `init` fails to create `_statusEl` (e.g., `_inputEl` missing parent), `_updateStatus` silently fails to announce search result counts to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2266: if (!_statusEl) { liveRegion.announce(`${count} suggestions available`); return; }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in `productFilter` `applyFilters` does not announce filter application if `_gridEl` is missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function in `productFilter` relies on `_gridEl` to trigger the animation and subsequent live region announcement. If `_gridEl` is null, the `liveRegion.announce` call inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback is never executed, leaving screen reader users unaware that filters have been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2858: if (!_gridEl) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.productsFiltered || '{n} product{s} shown'); return; }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in `testimonialsCarousel` `goTo` does not announce slide change if `_slides` is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The `goTo` function in `testimonialsCarousel` checks `if (!_slides.length) return;` before announcing the slide change. If the carousel is initialized with no slides, or slides are removed dynamically, the function exits without announcing the state, potentially leaving screen readers on stale content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3536: if (!_slides.length) { liveRegion.announce('No testimonials available'); return; }

issuesFound.title: Form validation function `validateField` in `inlineValidator` does not set `aria-invalid` if `errorEl` creation fails
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` calls `srUtils.markInvalid` which sets `aria-invalid="true"`. However, if `_getOrCreateErrorEl` fails to create or find an error element (e.g., DOM insertion fails), the validation state might not be fully applied or announced, as `markInvalid` relies on the error element for `aria-describedby`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3862: if (!errorEl) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); return; }

issuesFound.title: Form validation function `validate` in `contactForm` `_validateAll` does not clear `aria-invalid` on previously invalid fields if they become valid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in `contactForm` iterates through `CONTACT_FIELDS` and calls `inlineValidator.validate(field)`. While `inlineValidator` handles individual field state, `_validateAll` does not explicitly ensure `aria-invalid="false"` is set on fields that were previously invalid but are now valid before submission, relying solely on the blur/input triggers which may not have fired.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4077: CONTACT_FIELDS.forEach(cfg => { const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl); if (field) { const result = inlineValidator.validate(field); if (result && result.valid) field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); } });

issuesFound.title: Form validation function `validate` in `newsletterForm` `_onSubmit` does not clear `aria-invalid` on valid email before submission
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onSubmit` function in `newsletterForm` validates the email and focuses if invalid, but does not explicitly remove `aria-invalid="true"` if the field was previously marked invalid and is now valid upon submission attempt. This can cause screen readers to announce the field as invalid even when the submission proceeds.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3974: if (result.valid) { _emailEl && _emailEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }

issuesFound.title: `aria-expanded` not initialised on `_toggleEl` in `pricing` module if element exists but lacks attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module `init` function finds `_toggleEl` and adds `role="switch"` if missing, but does not explicitly set `aria-checked` (equivalent to `aria-expanded` for switches) to the initial state (`_pricingState.period === 'year'`) before the first interaction. If the HTML lacks this attribute, the initial state is unknown to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3280: if (_toggleEl && !_toggleEl.hasAttribute('aria-checked')) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: `aria-expanded` not initialised on `[data-view-mode]` buttons in `productFilter` module if element exists but lacks attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module `init` function calls `_syncTabAriaStates` for filter tabs but does not explicitly initialize `aria-pressed` on `[data-view-mode]` buttons if they are missing the attribute in the HTML. The `_onViewToggle` handler updates them on click, but the initial state remains undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2935: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { if (!b.hasAttribute('aria-pressed')) setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element `_bannerEl` in `cookieConsent` `_hideBanner` is removed from DOM without ensuring `aria-hidden` is set before removal
issuesFound.explanation: The `_hideBanner` function in `cookieConsent` calls `fadeOut` which eventually calls `removeElement`. While `removeElement` takes it out of the DOM, if the fade animation is interrupted or fails, the element might remain visible or focusable without `aria-hidden="true"` being explicitly set during the transition, potentially trapping focus or announcing hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4353: if (!_bannerEl) return; setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); if (_trapRelease) { _trapRelease(); _trapRelease = null; }

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element `_btn` in `scrollToTop` `_update` does not set `aria-hidden="true"` immediately when hiding
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function in `scrollToTop` removes the `CSS.visible` class and sets `hidden` attribute after a timeout, but sets `aria-hidden="true"` only inside that timeout. During the fade-out transition, the button remains in the accessibility tree, allowing screen reader users to interact with a visually disappearing element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: _visible = false; removeClass(_btn, CSS.visible); _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: User action `setPeriod` in `pricing` does not announce savings message update if `_savingsMsg` is missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in `pricing` calls `_updateSavingsMessage` which updates `_savingsMsg.textContent`. If `_savingsMsg` is null (element not found), the visual update fails, and no alternative announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the savings associated with the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3252: if (!_savingsMsg) { liveRegion.announce(`Save ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].discountPct}% with ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); return; }

issuesFound.title: User action `goTo` in `testimonialsCarousel` does not announce slide change if `liveRegion` is unavailable
issuesFound.explanation: The `goTo` function in `testimonialsCarousel` relies on `liveRegion.announce` to inform users of the slide change. If the `liveRegion` module fails to initialize (e.g., body not ready), the announcement is silently dropped, leaving screen reader users unaware that the carousel has moved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3558: try { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.carouselSlideAnnounce || 'Slide {n} of {total}').replace('{n}', String(target + 1)).replace('{total}', String(_slides.length))); } catch (e) { console.warn('Carousel announcement failed', e); }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function within `statCounterAnimator` updates the text content of the element (`el.textContent = _format(val, cfg)`) to display counting numbers. However, it does not write this change to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users will not be informed that the number is changing dynamically unless they refocus the element.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A (function statCounterAnimator._animate): Add a live region announcement in the `onComplete` or `onUpdate` callback. Example: `liveRegion.announce(\`Count updated to \${_format(val, cfg)}\`);`

issuesFound.title: Custom interactive element missing click handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind` function assigns `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` to `zoneEl`, making it a custom interactive control. It attaches a `keydown` listener for Enter/Space activation but fails to attach a `click` listener. Mouse users clicking the zone will not trigger the file input, blocking access for pointer users.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A (function fileUploadField.bind): Add a click listener to `zoneEl` that triggers the input. Example: `zoneEl.addEventListener('click', () => inputEl.click());`

issuesFound.title: Progress bar value changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateProgress` function updates `aria-valuenow` on the progress bar element to reflect wizard completion. However, the progress bar element is not given an `aria-live` attribute. Screen reader users are not notified when the progress value changes as they move between steps.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A (function multiStepWizard._updateProgress): Ensure the progress bar element has `aria-live="polite"`. Example: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');` (Add this in `init` where `_progressBar` is selected).

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls if target lacks ID
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function sets `aria-controls` on the trigger only if `_drawer.id` already exists (`if (... && _drawer.id)`). Unlike `faqAccordion` or `dropdownMenu`, it does not generate an ID for the drawer if one is missing. If the drawer lacks an ID, the relationship is not established, confusing assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A (function mobileNav.init): Ensure the drawer has an ID before setting aria-controls. Example: `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);`

fpTitles: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
Custom interactive element missing click handler
Progress bar value changes not announced
Toggle trigger missing aria-controls if target lacks ID

issuesFound.title: SELECTORS object missing critical keys for navigation and search
issuesFound.explanation: The `SELECTORS` configuration object is missing keys `primaryNav`, `navDrawer`, `navOverlay`, and `searchClear`. This causes `mobileNav.init` and `dropdownMenu` to fail to find the navigation drawer (silently exiting), blocking mobile menu access. It also causes `siteSearch` to fail to bind the clear button, preventing users from clearing search input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 228: Add the missing keys to the `SELECTORS` object:
  `primaryNav: '#primary-nav',` (or appropriate selector)
  `navDrawer: '#nav-drawer',` (or appropriate selector)
  `navOverlay: '#nav-overlay',` (or appropriate selector)
  `searchClear: '#search-clear',` (or appropriate selector)

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function toggles the visual `CSS.active` class on filter tab buttons but fails to update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users will not know which filter is currently active, as the ARIA state does not match the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1935: Update the loop to set `aria-pressed`:
  `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => {`
  `  const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory;`
  `  toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`
  `  setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`
  `});`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger but does not announce "Menu opened" to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that the dropdown panel has become visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1530: Add a live region announcement after opening:
  `function _openInstance(inst) {`
  `  if (inst.open) return;`
  `  inst.open = true;`
  `  inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  `  requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`
  `  liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  `  emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
  `}`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger but does not announce "Menu closed" to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that the dropdown panel has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: Add a live region announcement after closing:
  `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) {`
  `  if (!inst.open) return;`
  `  inst.open = false;`
  `  setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `  removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`
  `  liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`
  `  const dur = parseInt(getCssVar('--transition-base', document.documentElement) || '200', 10);`
  `  setTimeout(() => {`
  `    if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`
  `  }, dur + 20);`
  `  if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();`
  `  emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
  `}`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce dialog opening
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function displays a cookie consent dialog (`role="dialog"`) and traps focus, but does not announce "Cookie settings dialog opened" to screen readers. Users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3915: Add a live region announcement when the banner is shown:
  `function _showBanner() {`
  `  if (_bannerEl) return;`
  `  _bannerEl = qs('[data-cookie-banner]');`
  `  // ... (existing element creation code)`
  `  document.body.appendChild(_bannerEl);`
  `  // ... (existing event binding code)`
  `  setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`
  `  liveRegion.announce('Cookie settings dialog opened');`
  `  const trap = createFocusTrap(_bannerEl, { escapeDeactivates: false });`
  `  // ... (rest of function)`
  `}`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state

issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in `dropdownMenu` change the panel visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on `inst.trigger`. Users relying on screen readers will not know the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1836: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1856: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function sets `aria-expanded` to `'false'` in the `close` function but fails to set it to `'true'` when opening. This creates a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2676: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `close` functions expand/collapse content but do not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform users of the state change (e.g., "Answer expanded").
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2677: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));
  Line 2711: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount` function sets up ARIA attributes for the combobox but does not initialise `aria-expanded` to `'false'`. The input should indicate the suggestion list is closed on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the product count and empty state visibly but does not announce these changes to screen readers via `liveRegion.announce`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2381: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates price displays dynamically but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2551: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3356: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) controls the visibility of the nav drawer, but the `open()` and `close()` functions do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line buildMobileNav/open: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line buildMobileNav/close: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer is closed, the `close()` function removes the open CSS class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element (`_drawer`). If the drawer is hidden via CSS class removal rather than `display: none` or `hidden` attribute, it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line buildMobileNav/close: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function sets up event listeners for the mobile nav trigger but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. Assistive technologies may announce the state incorrectly on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line buildMobileNav/init: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Users relying on screen readers will not receive state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line buildDropdownMenu/_openInstance: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line buildDropdownMenu/_closeInstance: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the DOM but does not trigger a live region announcement. Users are not informed that a menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has invalid aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This attribute should only be used on the dialog element to indicate that content outside is inert. Placing it on the overlay is semantically incorrect and may confuse assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1693: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element with role="dialog" is missing the aria-modal="true" attribute. Without this, assistive technology may not properly convey that the dialog is modal and that interaction with content outside the dialog is disabled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} aria-modal="true" className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()} >

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby attribute references 'newsletter-email-error' when an error exists, but the FormGroup component does not render an element with this ID because it receives the 'error' prop instead of 'errorMessage' and 'errorId'. This leaves screen reader users without access to the specific error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1465: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error">
  Line 1475: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Contact name input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby attribute references 'contact-name-error' when an error exists, but the FormGroup component does not render an element with this ID due to prop name mismatch ('error' vs 'errorMessage'). This breaks the programmatic association between the input and its error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1553: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-name-error">
  Line 1566: aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Contact email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1569: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="contact-email-error">
  Line 1579: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text in feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: The links "Learn about the scanner", "Learn about the design checker", "Learn about keyboard testing", "Learn about the SR simulator", "Learn about guided remediation", "Learn about compliance reporting", "Learn about AI fix suggestions", and "Learn about continuous monitoring" use generic "Learn about" phrasing without the specific product name being immediately clear from the link text alone in all contexts. While the surrounding heading provides context, the link text out of context is insufficiently descriptive for users navigating by links list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 156: `axe Runner Pro scanner details` Line 165: `Contrast Studio design checker details` Line 174: `Focus Flow keyboard testing details` Line 183: `SR Simulator details` Line 192: `Guided remediation details` Line 201: `Compliance reporting details` Line 210: `AI fix suggestions details` Line 219: `Continuous monitoring details`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text in product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The links "View axe Runner Pro details", "View Contrast Studio details", "View Focus Flow details", "View VPAT Builder details", "View SR Simulator details", and "View CI Guard details" use generic "View" and "details" phrasing. This pattern fails to provide clear, specific destination context when read out of context in a screen reader links list, forcing users to rely on surrounding context that may not be available in AT navigation modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 256: `axe Runner Pro full specifications` Line 271: `Contrast Studio full specifications` Line 286: `Focus Flow full specifications` Line 301: `VPAT Builder full specifications` Line 316: `SR Simulator full specifications` Line 331: `CI Guard full specifications`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text in pricing section
issuesFound.explanation: The links "Get started for free", "Start Pro free trial", and "Talk to sales" lack specific context about the destination page when viewed in a links list. "Get started" and "Start" are generic action verbs that do not identify the specific plan or resulting action, while "Talk to sales" is vague regarding the contact method or form destination.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: `Select Starter plan` Line 365: `Select Pro plan free trial` Line 378: `Contact enterprise sales team`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text in hero section
issuesFound.explanation: The links "Start your free 14-day trial" and "Watch the 3-minute demo" within the hero section, while descriptive in isolation, rely on additional sr-only text to provide complete context. However, the visible text "Watch the 3-minute demo" does not clearly indicate the video destination or that it opens a media player, and "Start your free 14-day trial" could be more specific about the resulting action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 116: `Begin 14-day free trial registration` Line 119: `Watch product demonstration video`

issuesFound.title: Link with non-descriptive visible text in footer
issuesFound.explanation: The link "Cookie settings" uses the generic word "settings" which, when encountered in a links list out of context, does not clearly indicate that the link controls cookie preferences or opens a dialog. The visible text must provide the link purpose without relying on surrounding context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480: `Manage cookie preferences`

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text in FAQ section
issuesFound.explanation: The link "billing@luminarystudio.io" within the FAQ answer provides only the email address as link text. While the address itself is the destination, it lacks descriptive context about the purpose (e.g., "Contact billing department at billing@luminarystudio.io"), making it less clear when encountered in a links list that this is the appropriate contact for refunds.
issuesFound.fix: Line 449: `Email billing@luminarystudio.io for refund requests`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator (Footer navigation)

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers: .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the class and will cause screen-reader-only text to be displayed visually, disrupting the layout and potentially confusing sighted users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 444: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), and the :active state uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card class includes transform in its transition (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)), and the :hover state uses transform: translateY(-3px). No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this vertical movement, which can trigger vestibular disorders in sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border in the same rule. While outline-offset is present, it applies to a zero-width outline and renders nothing visible. Keyboard users navigating through the page will not see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring from buttons when they receive keyboard focus, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify the focused element.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24px. Additionally, the padding is set to 0, providing no buffer to increase the touch area. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px with a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to tap for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 36px, which is below the recommended 44×44px target size. The vertical padding is only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), which is less than the 8px needed to compensate and ensure an adequate touch target area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the minimum WCAG requirement but falls below the recommended 44px target size for optimal accessibility. No padding is defined in the rule to increase the touch area, making the links potentially difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which keeps the content in the document flow and visible on screen. It fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. The class should use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip properties to hide content visually without removing it from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement animation when the button is interacted with. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion, which can cause vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transform transition for the translateY property, creating spatial movement when the link receives focus. Users who have requested reduced motion may experience vestibular discomfort from this animation, and there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on hover and active states. This animation lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override, which can cause issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color changes (from neutral-700 to brand-primary) to distinguish them from surrounding text on hover. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as users who cannot perceive color differences will be unable to identify the links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove underlines and only change color on hover (from neutral-400 to neutral-0). issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Only outline-offset is specified, which renders no visible focus ring when outline width is zero. This removes keyboard focus indicators from all interactive elements that do not explicitly override it, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) and blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class modifier only adjusts outline-offset to -3px but does not restore a visible outline width or style. Since the global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this selector leaves focus indicators invisible for elements using this class, failing WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector only changes outline-color but does not set outline-width or outline-style. With outline: none inherited from the global :focus-visible rule, no focus ring is rendered despite the colour declaration, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px with zero padding. While this meets the absolute minimum of 24px, it falls below the 44px recommended target size without compensating spacing, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit dimensions of 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the control difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This contradicts the intended purpose of screen-reader-only text and may cause layout issues while failing to properly hide content visually (WCAG 1.1.1, 1.3.1).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast)). The :active state applies transform: translateY(1px), creating spatial movement that is not disabled under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The pseudo-element has a transition on the transform property (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)), which animates the arrow movement on hover (translateX(3px)). This spatial motion lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override, failing WCAG 2.3.3 for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property --colour-text-secondary is set to #aaaaaa. When applied to text on the default white background (--colour-bg-page: #ffffff), the contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. This affects paragraph text in section headers, hero sections, and other content areas.
issuesFound.fix: Line 26: --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for neutral-400 text
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property --colour-neutral-400 is set to #94a3b8. When used on the subtle background --colour-neutral-50 (#f8fafc), the contrast ratio is approximately 2.4:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1). This affects the logo bar labels and other secondary text elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 18: --colour-neutral-400: #64748b;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links (nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a) have text-decoration: none and rely solely on a colour change (var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)) to indicate hover state. Without an underline, border-bottom, or background highlight, colour alone is used to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global a rule and provide only a colour change on hover, with no underline, border, or background-color change to distinguish them from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 564: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change (var(--colour-neutral-400) to var(--colour-neutral-0)) on hover, with no additional visual differentiator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1314: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will have no visible indication of which element is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible without a replacement. In Windows High Contrast Mode, this removes the focus indicator entirely, leaving keyboard users without any focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358-361: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement. The site logo link will have no focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538-540: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on mobile nav toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. This hamburger menu button will lack a visible focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 553-555: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px but provides no visible replacement. Dropdown menu links will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596-599: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement. The search button will have no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625-627: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Account links in the header will lack focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 644-647: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes outline without providing a replacement focus indicator. "Learn more" links within feature cards will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 808-810: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Filter toggle buttons will lack a focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 926-928: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement. Monthly/annual billing toggle buttons will have no visible focus state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1022-1024: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible alternative. Accordion trigger buttons will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1146-1148: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement. The scrollable table region will lack a visible focus indicator when focused via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090-1092: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Links in the footer brand section will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1237-1239: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible removes outline without replacement. Footer navigation links will lack visible focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250-1252: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Social media links in the footer will not show focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1263-1265: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 502: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon buttons will be difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514-515: min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px. At 20px square, this is below the 24px minimum target size, making checkboxes difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0. While this meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended size and has no padding to compensate, potentially causing activation difficulties on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 616: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible. Additionally, it lacks proper clipping, causing screen-reader-only text to be visible or incorrectly positioned.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382-392: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435-440: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script) — Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } } (removing transform)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which tightens text and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word-spacing is not overridden by any media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or set to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on a background of var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.3:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum (4.5:1 required for normal text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1241: color: var(--colour-neutral-0);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555) background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1246: color: var(--colour-neutral-0);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.3:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1268: color: var(--colour-neutral-200);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on the dark footer background (#555555). The 1.9:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG 1.4.3 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1258: color: var(--colour-neutral-0);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight. Links rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: text-decoration: underline; OR add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to link selectors alongside the text-decoration: none;

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is announced via a live region, but the input field itself does not have aria-invalid set to "true". Screen reader users therefore cannot identify which specific field contains the invalid value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3530: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line 3536: Add removeAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid'); (inside the if (result.valid) block before disabling the input)

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hide function missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is hidden via CSS (likely transformed off-screen), the element remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set to "true". Screen reader users can still navigate to the hidden header content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: Add setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader show function missing aria-hidden removal
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is shown after being hidden, the aria-hidden attribute is not removed, so the header remains inaccessible to screen readers even though it is visually visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2859: Add removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: pricing comparison table trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table expand/collapse trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded on page load. If the table starts in a collapsed state (hidden), the trigger lacks aria-expanded="false", violating the disclosure pattern requirements.

fpTitles: discountCodeInput validation missing aria-invalid attribute
stickyHeader hide function missing aria-hidden attribute
stickyHeader show function missing aria-hidden removal
pricing comparison table trigger missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner prevents Escape key dismissal
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner is implemented as a modal dialog (role="dialog" with aria-modal="true") that traps focus, but explicitly disables the Escape key for closing (escapeDeactivates: false). This prevents keyboard users from dismissing the banner using the standard Escape key, forcing them to navigate to and activate a specific button, which degrades the experience and violates standard dialog behavior expectations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3374: const trap = createFocusTrap(_bannerEl, { escapeDeactivates: true });

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications use assertive alert role for non-error messages
issuesFound.explanation: All toast notifications are created with role="alert", which is an assertive live region that interrupts screen reader speech immediately. Success, warning, and info messages should use role="status" (polite) so they do not disrupt the user, while only critical errors should use role="alert".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: role: type === 'error' ? 'alert' : 'status',

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons have invalid aria-selected attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Quarterly/Annual) are implemented as buttons with aria-pressed to indicate the active state. However, the code also sets aria-selected, which is not a valid attribute on button elements (it is only valid on certain widget roles like tab, option, gridcell). This creates invalid ARIA markup that may confuse assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2548: Remove the line `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` entirely, relying solely on aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state.

issuesFound.title: File upload drop zone contains structural elements inside a button role
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone is given role="button" to make it appear as a clickable button to assistive technologies. However, the zone contains child elements including a <ul> list (the file list). ARIA button elements cannot contain interactive children or structural list elements; children of a button are considered presentational and hidden from the accessibility tree, which would hide the uploaded file list from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3630: Remove role="button" from the zoneEl. Instead, ensure the actual clickable area (or the zone itself if it must be a button) does not contain the list. The list should be a sibling of the zone, not a child, or the zone should not have role="button".

issuesFound.title: Contact form error summary container lacks alert role
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, an error summary box is created and focused to alert the user. However, the container is created without role="alert" or "status", so screen readers may not automatically announce the error summary content when it appears, even though it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3196: Add `role: 'alert',` to the createElement attributes object for the error summary div.

issuesFound.title: Discount code status message lacks live region role
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input displays success or error messages in a status element. While the code sets aria-live="assertive" dynamically, the element lacks a role attribute (e.g., role="alert" or "status"). Assistive technologies benefit from an explicit role to correctly interpret the importance of the message, especially since the live region is dynamically injected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2717: Add `_statusEl.setAttribute('role', result.valid ? 'status' : 'alert');` immediately after setting aria-live.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed (via click, hover, or keyboard), the functions _openInstance and _closeInstance update aria-expanded but do not write to an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has expanded or collapsed, leaving them unaware that content is now available or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1904 (inside _openInstance, after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
        Line 1923 (inside _closeInstance, after setAttr): `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Contact and newsletter email inputs lack autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The JavaScript queries personal data input fields (names and email addresses) but does not ensure they have HTML autocomplete attributes (e.g., autocomplete="email", "given-name", "family-name"). Without these attributes, browser autocomplete and assistive technologies cannot properly identify the purpose of the inputs, hindering user experience for cognitively impaired users and those relying on autocomplete.
issuesFound.fix:
In the init functions for newsletterForm (line ~3137) and contactForm (line ~3153), add attributes:
        Line for newsletter: `_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
        Lines for contact: `qs(SELECTORS.contactFirstName).setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`
        `qs(SELECTORS.contactLastName).setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');`
        `qs(SELECTORS.contactEmail).setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Image audit utility masks missing alt text by setting empty alt
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.auditImages function detects images missing alt attributes and "fixes" them by setting alt="". While this prevents validation errors, it effectively hides potentially informative images from screen readers by marking them as decorative. This masks underlying accessibility issues in the HTML rather than ensuring the images have meaningful alternative text, potentially hiding important content from users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1373: Instead of setting alt="", add the image to a list of violations to be reported to the developer console (beyond just debug warn) and do not modify the alt attribute, or set a conspicuous placeholder like alt="[Missing description]" to alert content creators.

issuesFound.title: Wizard form name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form name input (selector [name="wizard_name"]) collects personal data but lacks autocomplete attributes. This prevents autofill assistance for users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7895: After selecting the field in STEP_FIELDS configuration, add code to set `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form email input (selector [name="wizard_email"]) collects personal data but lacks autocomplete attributes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7896: Set `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard form card name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form cardholder name input (selector [name="wizard_card_name"]) collects payment personal data but lacks autocomplete attributes for credit card names.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7897: Set `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');`

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle buttons missing keyboard operability
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher attaches click event listeners to elements matching [data-theme-toggle] but does not add keyboard event handlers for Enter or Space keys. If these elements are not native <button> elements in the HTML, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 9855: Add keydown event listener to handle Enter and Space keys, or ensure elements have role="button" and tabindex="0" and add keydown handler: `btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); set(newTheme); } });`

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons missing keyboard operability
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter module delegates click events to [data-view-mode] elements to toggle between grid and list views. If these elements are not native buttons, they lack keyboard accessibility as the code does not provide Enter/Space key handlers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4385: Add keyboard event delegation for Enter and Space keys on these elements, or ensure they are native buttons with click handlers.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (selector #scroll-top-btn) is assigned aria-hidden states but never receives an accessible name (aria-label). Screen reader users will not know the purpose of this button when it becomes visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2825: Add `_btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing period switch missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (selector [data-billing-toggle]) is assigned role="switch" and aria-checked, but no accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby) is programmatically set. Screen reader users will not understand what this switch controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4925: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', 'Billing period');` or reference a visible label.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (referenced via SELECTORS.searchClear) has a click handler attached but no accessible name is set programmatically. If the button contains only an icon or "×" character without text, screen reader users will not know its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3855: After selecting the clear button, add `_clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation hamburger button (selector .nav-toggle) receives aria-expanded and aria-controls attributes, but no accessible name (aria-label) is set to indicate its purpose (e.g., "Open navigation menu").
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu');`

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner close/decline button is created with text content, but if the button uses only an icon or symbol without visible text, it lacks an aria-label. The code sets textContent but doesn't ensure aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8725: Ensure the decline button has `aria-label="Reject non-essential cookies"` or similar descriptive text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern and leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", so screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that show the active filter state but do not update aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state, failing to communicate the active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { ... })` loop, after the line `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field, which is required for screen readers to identify the invalid state (affects newsletterForm, contactForm, and multiStepWizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While opening the navigation menu is announced to screen readers via liveRegion.announce, closing it is not announced, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed and focus has returned to the trigger.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the live region, leaving users unaware that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance, after setAttr): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line (in _closeInstance, after setAttr): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles the auto-play state but does not update aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state (playing vs paused), failing to communicate the current toggle state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` at the end of the togglePlay function.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the user toggles auto-play, the change in state (playing vs paused) is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether auto-play is active or not.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in play(), end of function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Auto-play started');`
  Line (in pause(), end of function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Auto-play paused');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens but does not activate a focus trap (the _trap variable is declared but never initialized or activated), allowing keyboard users to tab out of the menu into the background content while the menu is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes to display the drawer but omits updating the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown panel removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true", leaving assistive technologies unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function, after the early return check.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ answer animates the panel and updates CSS classes, but the trigger button lacks aria-expanded="true", so screen readers do not announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from identifying the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible UI but neither function writes to an aria-live region, so users of assistive technologies are not notified of the menu’s visibility change.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after opening. In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after closing.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation for drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: In init(), after _drawer is assigned, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });`. In open(), after showing the drawer, add `_trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field collects personal data (email address) but the JavaScript does not ensure the autocomplete attribute is set to "email", preventing browsers from autofill assistance.
issuesFound.fix: After `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']);`, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input handles personal data but lacks programmatic assurance of the autocomplete="email" attribute in the JavaScript enhancement layer.
issuesFound.fix: In the init() function, after binding validation to the email field, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');` where _emailEl is the email input.

issuesFound.title: Contact form name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The full name input for personal data does not have autocomplete="name" ensured by the JavaScript, failing WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: After binding validation to the full_name field, add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s name input collects personal data but the JavaScript does not set autocomplete="name", hindering autofill.
issuesFound.fix: In the init() function’s STEP_FIELDS binding loop, for the wizard_name selector, add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s email input lacks autocomplete="email" in the JavaScript layer, failing to support autofill for personal data.
issuesFound.fix: In the init() function’s STEP_FIELDS binding loop, for the wizard_email selector, add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
Contact form name input missing autocomplete attribute
