issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and emits an event, but it never calls setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true') on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after setting _open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the panel is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey the error state explicitly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1540: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); before or after the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While the close() function sets aria-expanded="false", it does not announce the navigation closure via the live region. Users navigating with screen readers may not realize the menu has closed, especially when focus remains on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2190: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); before the closing brace of the function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle the active category visually via CSS classes, but the function does not set aria-pressed (or aria-selected) to indicate which filter is currently active. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3353: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch Escape key handler missing announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users press Escape to close the search suggestions, the _close() function is called without announcing the closure to screen readers. This leaves users unaware that the suggestion panel has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3102: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchClosed || 'Search closed'); after _close(); and before the break statement.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never assigned a createFocusTrap() instance in the init() function. Consequently, the condition if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) in open() always fails, and focus is never trapped within the mobile navigation drawer, violating WCAG 2.4.3 for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130 (in init function): Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); after verifying _drawer exists.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard transitions between steps but does not implement a focus trap to contain keyboard navigation within the active step panel. Users can tab out of the wizard into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 for multi-step forms.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4520: Add const trap = createFocusTrap(_steps[_current].panelEl, { escapeDeactivates: false }); and activate it in _goToStep when showing a new step.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown via the Escape key (which calls _closeInstance with returnFocus), no live region announcement is made. While focus returns to the trigger, users may not understand that the panel has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2310: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed'); before emitting the dropdown:close event.

fpTitles: multiStepWizard missing focus trap

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to indicate the dropdown's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1862: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1872: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states to filter products, but the function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed (or aria-selected) to convey the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2604: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, preventing screen readers from identifying the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1012: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving screen readers thinking the field is still invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1029: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`) at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and the navigation is closed, violating the requirement that toggle buttons must indicate their initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1833: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's id, preventing screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1834: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after ensuring the drawer has an id.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly/quarterly/annual), the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers to inform users of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2799: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', period));` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, if it uses off-screen positioning (CSS transform/translate) rather than display:none, it remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true", allowing screen readers to navigate to hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before or after `_unlockScroll();`. Also add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in the open() function when opening.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed. The open() function announces when opening, but close() lacks a corresponding announcement, creating an asymmetric experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close(): Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by setting CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but it does not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that select categories, but the _syncTabAriaStates function only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which tab is currently active or pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(): Inside the forEach loop, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Form validation helper does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid and markValid functions in srUtils manage error message associations via aria-describedby, but they do not set or clear the aria-invalid attribute on the form field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce field validity to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): After addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); Line srUtils.markValid(): Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a FAQ item, the open function animates the panel and sets the open state, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent accessibility state where screen readers cannot detect when items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): After addClass(item.el, CSS.open); add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters and updating the product grid, the function updates the count badge and empty state visually, but it does not announce the results to screen reader users via a live region. Users do not know how many products match the current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match your filters`);

issuesFound.title: pricing module does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods between monthly and annual, the prices update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen reader users informing them that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After updating the plan cards, add liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users. Users navigating with keyboards may not realize the menu has appeared or disappeared, especially if the dropdown does not receive focus immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips does not announce filter removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user clicks a filter chip to remove it, the filter is removed and the product grid updates, but there is no announcement confirming that the filter was removed or what filters remain active. Screen reader users lose context.
issuesFound.fix: Line activeFilterChips._onChipClick(): After the switch statement handling removal, add liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, if the drawer remains in the DOM with off-screen positioning (such as transform: translateX), it remains in the accessibility tree. The close function should set aria-hidden="true" to remove it from the accessibility tree until opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close(): After removeClass(_drawer, ...); add _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); Line mobileNav.open(): Add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop focus fallback may fail
issuesFound.explanation: The function attempts to focus the skipLink or document.body after scrolling, but document.body may not be focusable if it doesn't have tabindex="-1". This could result in focus being lost or remaining on the clicked button.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick(): Ensure the focus target is valid by checking if (returnEl && returnEl.focus) and ensuring document.body has tabindex="-1" if used as fallback.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step focus may fail
issuesFound.explanation: The function attempts to focus the firstHeading in the new step, but if no heading exists in the panel, focus is not moved to the step panel. This can leave keyboard users with focus in an unexpected location or outside the wizard.
issuesFound.fix: Line multiStepWizard._goToStep(): Add fallback after firstHeading check: if (!firstHeading) { entering.panelEl.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); entering.panelEl.focus(); }

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField errors not associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails, an error message is announced via liveRegion, but the error is not programmatically associated with the file input via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage. Screen reader users may not understand which field has the error when navigating back to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line fileUploadField._addFile(): When validation fails, after creating the error message, call srUtils.addDescribedBy(binding.inputEl, errorEl) and setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mediaPlayerController overwrites existing tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The function sets tabindex="0" on media elements without checking if a tabindex already exists. This could overwrite intentional negative tabindex values that were meant to exclude the element from the tab order or manage focus differently.
issuesFound.fix: Line mediaPlayerController.bind(): Change setAttr(media, 'tabindex', media.getAttribute('tabindex') || '0'); to only set if not already present, or check if media.hasAttribute('tabindex') before setting.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Although the register() function initializes aria-expanded to "false", the state is never toggled when the dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2336 (inside _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2352 (inside _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates the visual state, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3716 (inside open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function queries the toggle button and drawer elements but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button when the page loads. This prevents screen readers from knowing the initial collapsed state of the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2244 (after _initialised = true): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and ensure the trigger has an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer: `if (_drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds and removes error messages and CSS classes for valid and invalid states, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the form field. Consequently, screen readers cannot identify which fields contain validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3116 (inside _renderResult): In the `if (result.valid)` block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`. In the `else` block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and the count badge when filters change, but it does not announce the results (e.g., "Showing 5 of 20 products") via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter action completed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680 (inside applyFilters, within the onDone callback of filterAnimation.run): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates the displayed prices across all plan cards when the billing period changes (e.g., monthly to annual), but it does not announce this change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies are unaware that the pricing information has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2937 (inside setPeriod, after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not activate focus trap or move focus
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function declares a focus trap variable (_trap) but never activates it. Additionally, it does not move keyboard focus into the navigation drawer when it opens. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens off-screen or behind them, and they can tab outside the drawer into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2176 (inside open function): After opening the drawer, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and move focus to the first focusable element: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function deactivates the focus trap but does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened the menu. When the drawer closes, focus is lost (dropped to the body or top of the page), disorienting keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200 (inside close function, at the end): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handler for non-native triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion._onKeydown handler manages Arrow keys, Home, and End for keyboard navigation, but it does not handle Enter or Space keys. The faqRegistry.parse() function defensively adds role="button" and tabindex="0" to triggers that are not native buttons, but without Enter/Space handling, these triggers are not operable by keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3756 (inside _onKeydown): Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `e.preventDefault(); toggle(item);`.

fpTitles: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handler for non-native triggers

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating the requirement that toggle buttons must communicate their initial collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the null check for `_trigger` and `_drawer`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, leaving keyboard users at the trigger. When it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger, causing focus loss.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: Add after `_open = true;`: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`
  Line mobileNav.close: Add before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...`: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons open and close panels via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they never update aria-expanded to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Newly registered dropdown triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false", so assistive technologies assume the dropdown is not expandable or fail to communicate its initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input lacks aria-expanded="false" on initial page load, failing to communicate to assistive technologies that it controls a collapsible suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons indicate the active filter only through visual styling. They do not expose their pressed state via aria-pressed, making it impossible for screen reader users to know which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: Inside the forEach, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When inline validation detects an error, it updates the visual styling and error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. When the field becomes valid, it fails to clear aria-invalid. Assistive technologies cannot convey the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`
  Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates and the count badge changes visually, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match their selection or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products match your filter\`);` immediately after the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: pricing module not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the displayed prices visually, but the price change and new billing period are not announced to screen reader users, who may not realize the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);` immediately after the line `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden visually via CSS classes but may remain in the accessibility tree (depending on CSS implementation). If the drawer uses transform or visibility to hide, screen readers can still navigate to it when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line mobileNav.close: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

fpTitles: mobileNav missing focus management on open/close

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not reset to "false". Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1890: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `_open = false;` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users cannot perceive that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2030: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not set aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input does not set aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list closes, leaving assistive technology users unaware that the list has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3595: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _close after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle the active category but only update CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed attributes to indicate which filter is currently active to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4225: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period buttons (monthly/annual) toggle the pricing display but only update CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed to indicate the selected period to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4845: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2785: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside markInvalid after addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes, the function removes error descriptions but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid states announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of markValid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen reader users may not realize the menu is collapsible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after checking elements exist and before adding event listeners.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID, preventing screen reader users from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1936: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after ensuring the drawer has an ID.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Newly registered dropdown triggers are not initialized with aria-expanded="false", so screen reader users may not perceive them as expandable controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls in the register function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", leaving assistive technology users unaware that it controls a collapsible suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3640: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after mounting the suggestion list and setting other ARIA attributes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can Tab out of the menu into the background content, creating a modal dialog accessibility barrier.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1875: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` before emitting the open event.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates visually and the count badge changes, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users of the new result count or filter state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4145: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');` after _updateCountBadge call.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle billing periods, prices update visually across all cards, but screen reader users receive no confirmation of the price change or the new billing period selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4780: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu changes the visual page state significantly, but no polite live region announcement informs screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1877 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line 1902 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ answers changes visible content, but the functions do not announce the state change to screen reader users via the available live region utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4540 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`. Line 4565 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to style error states but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed the `errorId` prop, so the error message element is never rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference that fails WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1113-1121: 
    <FormGroup
      label="Email address"
      htmlFor="newsletter-email"
      errorId="newsletter-email-error"
      errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
    >
      <TextInput
        id="newsletter-email"
        type="email"
        name="email"
        placeholder="you@company.com"
        autoComplete="email"
        value={email.state.value}
        onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
        onBlur={email.onBlur}
        required
        invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
        aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
      />
    </FormGroup>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup lacks the `errorId` prop, so the error element is never rendered. This breaks the ARIA relationship and fails WCAG 4.1.2.

      label="Full name"

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop (per its TypeScript interface), but the carousel's previous and next buttons are rendered without this prop, resulting in icon-only buttons with no accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of these controls.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm field error messages not connected to input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props to render and associate error messages, but NewsletterForm passes an error prop that does not exist in the interface. Consequently, error messages never render and the input's aria-describedby references a non-existent ID ('newsletter-email-error'), breaking the connection between the field and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm field error messages not connected to inputs
issuesFound.explanation: All four FormGroup instances in ContactForm pass an error prop that is not defined in the component interface, preventing error messages from rendering. Additionally, the aria-describedby attributes on the inputs reference IDs (e.g., 'contact-name-error') that are never rendered into the DOM, creating broken ARIA references.
issuesFound.fix: Line: For each FormGroup, replace error={...} with errorId="contact-{field}-error" errorMessage={field.state.touched ? field.state.error : undefined}

issuesFound.fix: Line 840: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active page link in mobile navigation is indicated visually but not programmatically with aria-current, failing to communicate the current location to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Decorative cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integrations for visual marquee effect is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Hero CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The group of call-to-action buttons lacks an aria-label, making it unclear to screen reader users what the purpose of this button group is when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: <div className="hero-section__ctas" aria-label="Call to action links">

issuesFound.title: Trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The row of trust and certification badges is a distinct group but lacks an aria-label to identify it as a "Trust and certification badges" region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust and certification badges">

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters are read by screen readers in addition to the container's aria-label, causing redundant announcements. The decorative star symbols should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not marked with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not visible are still in the accessibility tree, causing screen readers to read content from hidden slides and creating confusion about the slide order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1034: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation section toggle buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that expand/collapse submenu sections in the mobile nav have aria-controls but lack aria-expanded to indicate their state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1284: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure buttons in the FAQ accordion control the visibility of answer panels but do not communicate their expanded/collapsed state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1576: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop (per its TypeScript interface) to provide an accessible name, but none is provided. The button contains only an emoji character.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1312: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only close button in the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label, leaving screen reader users without context for the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1267: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the character "‹" and lacks an aria-label, making its purpose unclear to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1632: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the character "›" and lacks an aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1637: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but no element with that ID is rendered in the component. Screen readers will not announce the field's error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1744: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} /> AND add errorId="newsletter-email-error" to the FormGroup props at line ~1734.

issuesFound.title: Contact form name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby attribute references 'contact-name-error', but FormGroup is not passed an errorId prop, so the error message element is never rendered with that ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1841: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} /> AND add errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup at line ~1835.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby references 'contact-email-error', but the error message element is not rendered with this ID because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1858: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined} /> AND add errorId="contact-email-error" to FormGroup at line ~1852.

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby references 'contact-subject-error', but the corresponding error element is not rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1876: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} /> AND add errorId="contact-subject-error" to FormGroup at line ~1870.

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-describedby references 'contact-message-error', but the error element is not rendered because FormGroup is missing the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1896: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} /> AND add errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup at line ~1888.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether it is the currently selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1510: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button lacks aria-pressed to indicate its selection state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1518: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements (SkipLinks, DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer), but the SkipLinks nav lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~572: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other nav regions when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1054: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label to identify it as a landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1346: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Statistics section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1380: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan cards missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan articles are repeated in a grid but lack aria-labelledby to connect them to their heading text, making them indistinguishable when navigating by article landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1540: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required indicator "*" is not hidden from assistive technology and may be announced as "star" by screen readers, which is confusing when the word "required" is also announced via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~891: *

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~827: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link (when item.current is true) visually indicates the current page but lacks aria-current="page" to communicate this state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1075: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link in the mobile menu lacks aria-current="page" to indicate the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1300: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Star rating widget missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The read-only star rating container has an aria-label describing the rating value but lacks role="img", which is required for the aria-label to be properly exposed to assistive technology on a span element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~596: <span role="img" aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`}>
